topic.what: Three-layer quantum circuit integrity framework combining SIS (Structural Integrity Score), OIS (Operational Integrity Score via Jensen-Shannon distance), and IGS (Interaction Graph Semantic-Logical Score)
topic.how: Combines structural metrics (gate count depth topology), interaction-level graph analysis, and behavioral output distribution comparison to detect anomalies under controlled injection
topic.why_matters: Addresses incomplete assessment from single-perspective validation in NISQ era where circuits undergo compilation transformations and potential adversarial modifications

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of anomalies detected when SIS >= 0.95 (structural blind-spots)
characteristics.vs_baseline: SIS alone fails to detect these anomalies
characteristics.evidence: Table IV - 569 blind-spot cases from 688 anomalous samples
characteristics.context: Structural analysis misses behavioral and interaction-level deviations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of anomalous circuits where SIS remains >= 0.95 despite injected anomalies
characteristics.evidence: Section V-D - 569 of 688 samples at SIS threshold
characteristics.context: Majority of anomalies preserve structural properties enough to evade SIS detection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Correlation between interaction-level and behavioral similarity
characteristics.evidence: Table V - weak correlation decreases with severity
characteristics.context: Interaction-level similarity does not guarantee behavioral equivalence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational cost comparison between IGS and OIS
characteristics.value: IGS stable low; OIS high and variable beyond 10 qubits
characteristics.context: Pre-execution IGS maintains consistent runtime while OIS scales exponentially

characteristics.what_itasures: Behavioral deviation detection across all anomaly types
characteristics.value: Consistently decreases with severity across all anomaly types
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 2b - captures gate substitution/reordering deviations missed by SIS
characteristics.context: Behavioral evaluation catches structural blind-spot cases
